summary:
Claim: A 4-year-old with 0.2 ppm fluoride in drinking water should receive systemic fluoride supplementation rather than topical agents.  
Evidence: - Environmental fluoride at 0.2 ppm falls below recommended thresholds for caries prevention. - Topical fluoride (toothpaste/rinse) poses high ingestion risk in children under 6 years, increasing fluorosis risk.  
Answer: A
